sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 18.107 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.18)

statement.latex: Is there a finitely generated infinite residually finite$p$-group such that every subgroup of infinite index is cyclic? The answer is known to be negative for$p=2$. Note that in (M.\,Ershov, A.\,Jaikin-Zapirain, J.~Reine Angew. Math., { 677} (2013), 71--134) it is shown that for every prime$p$there is a finitely generated infinite residually finite$p$-group such that every finitely generated subgr...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
